violations.text: Popcorn chicken and cheese were observed held at 56- and 46-degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the rear ice cream cooler vent have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Chicken patties were observed held at 42-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector observed the pan was filled above the fill line; PIC disposed of chicken patties that were above 41 degrees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

Ice cream cooler located in the front service area was not in good repair. Inspector observed a piece of plastic Moulding missing. Gasket on Coke cooler was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -Coke refrigerator door -vents of ice cream coolers. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The following areas within the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: -ventilation hoods wall behind menu board -floor under front ice cream cooler -floors throughout were observed to be sticky (PIC stated it is residue from new floor cleaner). The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
